class Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel, metaclass=PageBase):
    title = models.CharField(
        verbose_name=_("title"),
        max_length=255,
        help_text=_("The page title as you'd like it to be seen by the public"),
    )
    title.required_on_save = True
    # to reflect title of a current draft in the admin UI
    draft_title = models.CharField(max_length=255, editable=False)
    slug = models.SlugField(
        verbose_name=_("slug"),
        allow_unicode=True,
        max_length=255,
        help_text=_(
            "The name of the page as it will appear in URLs e.g http://domain.com/blog/[my-slug]/"
        ),
    )
    content_type = models.ForeignKey(
        ContentType,
        verbose_name=_("content type"),
        related_name="pages",
        on_delete=models.SET(get_default_page_content_type),
    )
    content_type.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True
    url_path = models.TextField(verbose_name=_("URL path"), blank=True, editable=False)
    owner = models.ForeignKey(
        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,
        verbose_name=_("owner"),
        null=True,
        blank=True,
        editable=True,
        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
        related_name="owned_pages",
    )
    owner.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True

    seo_title = models.CharField(
        verbose_name=_("title tag"),
        max_length=255,
        blank=True,
        help_text=_(
            "The name of the page displayed on search engine results as the clickable headline."
        ),
    )

    show_in_menus_default = False
    show_in_menus = models.BooleanField(
        verbose_name=_("show in menus"),
        default=False,
        help_text=_(
            "Whether a link to this page will appear in automatically generated menus"
        ),
    )
    search_description = models.TextField(
        verbose_name=_("meta description"),
        blank=True,
        help_text=_(
            "The descriptive text displayed underneath a headline in search engine results."
        ),
    )

    latest_revision_created_at = models.DateTimeField(
        verbose_name=_("latest revision created at"), null=True, editable=False
    )

    _revisions = GenericRelation(
        "wagtailcore.Revision",
        content_type_field="content_type",
        object_id_field="object_id",
        related_query_name="page",
        for_concrete_model=False,
    )

    # Override WorkflowMixin's GenericRelation to specify related_query_name
    # so we can do WorkflowState.objects.filter(page=...) queries.
    # There is no need to override the workflow_states property, as the default
    # implementation in WorkflowMixin already ensures that the queryset uses the
    # base Page content type.
    _workflow_states = GenericRelation(
        "wagtailcore.WorkflowState",
        content_type_field="base_content_type",
        object_id_field="object_id",
        related_query_name="page",
        for_concrete_model=False,
    )

    # When using a specific queryset, accessing the _workflow_states GenericRelation
    # will yield no results. This is because the _workflow_states GenericRelation
    # uses the base_content_type as the content_type_field, which is not the same
    # as the content type of the specific queryset. To work around this, we define
    # a second GenericRelation that uses the specific content_type to be used
    # when working with specific querysets.
    _specific_workflow_states = GenericRelation(
        "wagtailcore.WorkflowState",
        content_type_field="content_type",
        object_id_field="object_id",
        related_query_name="page",
        for_concrete_model=False,
    )

    # If non-null, this page is an alias of the linked page
    # This means the page is kept in sync with the live version
    # of the linked pages and is not editable by users.
    alias_of = models.ForeignKey(
        "self",
        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
        null=True,
        blank=True,
        editable=False,
        related_name="aliases",
    )
    alias_of.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True

    search_fields = [
        index.SearchField("title", boost=2),
        index.AutocompleteField("title"),

class AbstractPage(
    WorkflowMixin,

    MP_Node,
):
    """
    Abstract superclass for Page. According to Django's inheritance rules, managers set on
    abstract models are inherited by subclasses, but managers set on concrete models that are extended
    via multi-table inheritance are not. We therefore need to attach PageManager to an abstract
    superclass to ensure that it is retained by subclasses of Page.
    """

    objects = PageManager()

    class Meta:
        abstract = True

    ("lock_page", _("Lock"), _("Lock/unlock pages you've locked")),

]

]

from django.core import checks
from django.db import models
from django.utils.functional import cached_property
from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _

from wagtail.actions.publish_revision import PublishRevisionAction
from wagtail.actions.unpublish import UnpublishAction
from wagtail.locks import ScheduledForPublishLock

from .revisions import RevisionMixin


class DraftStateMixin(models.Model):
    live = models.BooleanField(verbose_name=_("live"), default=True, editable=False)
    has_unpublished_changes = models.BooleanField(
        verbose_name=_("has unpublished changes"), default=False, editable=False
    )

    first_published_at = models.DateTimeField(
        verbose_name=_("first published at"), blank=True, null=True, db_index=True
    )
    last_published_at = models.DateTimeField(
        verbose_name=_("last published at"), null=True, editable=False
    )
    live_revision = models.ForeignKey(
        "wagtailcore.Revision",
        related_name="+",
        verbose_name=_("live revision"),
        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
        null=True,
        blank=True,
        editable=False,
    )

    go_live_at = models.DateTimeField(
        verbose_name=_("go live date/time"), blank=True, null=True
    )
    expire_at = models.DateTimeField(
        verbose_name=_("expiry date/time"), blank=True, null=True
    )
    expired = models.BooleanField(
        verbose_name=_("expired"), default=False, editable=False
    )

    class Meta:
        abstract = True

    @classmethod
    def check(cls, **kwargs):
        return [
            *super().check(**kwargs),
            *cls._check_revision_mixin(),
        ]

    @classmethod
    def _check_revision_mixin(cls):
        mro = cls.mro()
        error = checks.Error(
            "DraftStateMixin requires RevisionMixin to be applied after DraftStateMixin.",
            hint="Add RevisionMixin to the model's base classes after DraftStateMixin.",

content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Here is the audit, worked through all seven steps.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Wagtail, a Django CMS (Python backend + React/TypeScript admin client). ~1,655 files, Django + React.

**Package layout** (all under `wagtail/`):
- `wagtail/models/` — core domain models, split one concern per file: `pages.py` (the Page model + its satellites), `sites.py`, `draft_state.py`, `revisions.py`, `locking.py`, `workflows.py`, `i18n.py`, `reference_index.py`, `view_restrictions.py`, `specific.py`.
- `wagtail/actions/` — command objects that mutate pages (move/copy/publish/delete/alias/translate).
- `wagtail/admin/` — the editor UI: `views/`, `forms/`, `api/`, `viewsets/`, `ui/`, `rich_text/` (heaviest area, 5,610 symbols).
- `wagtail/api/v2/` — public REST API.
- `wagtail/permission_policies/`, `wagtail/templatetags/`, `wagtail/management/commands/`, `wagtail/signal_handlers.py`, `wagtail/views.py` (front-end serving).
- `wagtail/contrib/` — optional subsystems: `redirects/`, `sitemaps/`, `frontend_cache/`, `forms/`, `routable_page/`, `search_promotions/`, `simple_translation/`, `settings/`.
- `client/src/`, `wagtail/test/`, `wagtail/tests/` — JS client, test apps, tests (out of scope for a production audit).

**The page model:** `class Page` at `wagtail/models/pages.py:289`, spanning lines 289–2125. It's the tree-node CMS page editors create/publish/serve. It's a hub — Sense reports **188 direct callers, 301 affected symbols across 48 files**.

---

## Step 2 — The Page model contract

`Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel, metaclass=PageBase)` — `wagtail/models/pages.py:289`. `AbstractPage` (`:248`) composes the mixins that supply most of the contract:
`WorkflowMixin, PreviewableMixin, DraftStateMixin, LockableMixin, RevisionMixin, TranslatableMixin, SpecificMixin, MP_Node`.

**Own fields** (`pages.py`):
- `title` `:290`, `draft_title` `:297`, `slug` `:298`, `seo_title` `:325`, `search_description` `:342`, `show_in_menus` `:335`
- `content_type` FK→ContentType `:306` (drives `.specific`), `url_path` (text, tree-derived) `:313`
- `owner` FK→User `:314`, `alias_of` self-FK `:392` (alias mechanic), `latest_revision_created_at` `:350`
- GenericRelations: `_revisions` `:354`, `_workflow_states` `:367`, `_specific_workflow_states` `:381`

**Tree mechanics** (from `MP_Node`, treebeard materialized path): `path`, `depth`, `numchild` — used everywhere via `get_parent()`, `get_children()`, `get_descendants()`, `move()`. `set_url_path(parent)` `:577` recomputes `url_path` from tree position.

**Publishing state** (from `DraftStateMixin`, `draft_state.py:13`): `live` `:14`, `has_unpublished_changes` `:15`, `first_published_at` `:19`, `last_published_at` `:22`, `live_revision` `:25`, `go_live_at` `:35`, `expire_at` `:38`, `expired` `:41`. Behavior via `Page.publish()` `:1219`.

**i18n** (TranslatableMixin): `locale`, `translation_key`. **Locking** (LockableMixin): `locked`, `locked_by`, `locked_at`.

**Search contract:** `search_fields` `:402` enumerates the indexed shape (`title`, `live`, `owner`, `content_type`, `path`, `depth`, `locked`, `show_in_menus`, `first_published_at`, `last_published_at`, `locale`, `translation_key`…). **Copy contract:** `default_exclude_fields_in_copy` `:435` names `id/depth/numchild/url_path/path/latest_revision/comments`.

**What a teammate must know about how other code holds page references — two coupling modes:**
1. **Stored reference** — satellite models with a page FK/PK in *their own* schema (survive across requests, migration-sensitive). Sense's `composed_by`/`affected_via_composition` lists these.
2. **Transient query** — code that does `Page.objects.…` / `.specific` / tree traversal per request (breaks at runtime if field names/tree semantics move).

---

## Step 3 — Every production dependent (flat, one per line, file:line)


---

## Step 4 — Grouped by subsystem

**Core model & satellite models** (`wagtail/models/`) — *store a page reference in their own schema*: `GroupPagePermission` `pages.py:2147`, `PageViewRestriction` `:2470`, `WorkflowPage` `:2537`, `PageLogEntry` `:2629`, `PageSubscription` `:2843` (all FK/PK to page); `Site.root_page` `sites.py:90` + `get_site_root_paths` `:218` (root-path cache keyed on tree `url_path`); `BasePageManager` `pages.py:152` (queryset contract).

**Reference index** (`wagtail/models/reference_index.py`) — *generic stored references*: `register_model` `:290`, `get_references_to` `:600`. Records every FK-to-page from any model in a side table.

**Page actions** (`wagtail/actions/`) — *mutate tree/publish state*: move `move_page.py:21`, copy `copy_page.py:31`, delete `delete_page.py:14`, unpublish `unpublish_page.py:17`, publish-revision `publish_page_revision.py:20`, alias `create_alias.py:29`/`convert_alias.py:22`, translate `copy_for_translation.py:30`, revert `revert_to_page_revision.py:20`.

**Front-end serving** (`wagtail/`) — *transient query per visitor request*: `serve` `views.py:16` (route by host+path via tree), `slugurl`/`pageurl` `templatetags/wagtailcore_tags.py:52`, `PageLinkHandler` `rich_text/pages.py:12,16` (resolve rich-text page links).

**Public REST API** (`wagtail/api/v2/views.py`) — `PagesAPIViewSet.get_root_page` `:512`, `get_base_queryset` `:518` (serializes `live`, tree filters `child_of`/`descendant_of`).

**Admin API & viewsets** (`wagtail/admin/api/`, `admin/viewsets/`): `PagesAdminAPIViewSet` `api/views.py:86,92`; `PageViewSetRegistry.get_content_type_id_by_page_id` `viewsets/pages.py:583`.

**Admin editor views/forms/UI** (`wagtail/admin/`): choosers `views/chooser.py:235,440,512`; move/copy/parent `views/pages/move.py:66`, `choose_parent.py:24`, `copy.py:46`, `bulk_actions/move.py:12,67`; reports `reports/locked_pages.py:59`, `reports/page_types_usage.py:27`; workflow queue `views/workflows.py:307`; status panel `ui/side_panels.py:268`; usage `models.py:30`; forms `forms/pages.py:15,178,262,283`; rich-text converters `rich_text/converters/editor_html.py:172`, `html_to_contentstate.py:234`.

**Permissions** (`wagtail/permission_policies/pages.py:162`) + `GroupPagePermission` — tree-scoped permission checks walk `path`/`depth`.

**Signal handlers** (`wagtail/signal_handlers.py`): `pre_delete_page_unpublish` `:31`, `post_delete_page_log_deletion` `:38`, reference-index sync `:66` — connected with `sender=Page` `:112`.

**Management commands** (`wagtail/management/commands/`): `fixtree.py:36`, `move_pages.py:12`, `set_url_paths.py:16` (rewrites `url_path` from tree), `publish_scheduled.py:29` (publishes on `go_live_at`).

**Contrib subsystems** (`wagtail/contrib/`): redirects (`redirect_page` FK `redirects/models.py:33`, published-page signals `redirects/signal_handlers.py`); frontend_cache (`page_published`/`page_unpublished` handlers `frontend_cache/signal_handlers.py:7,11`); sitemaps (`sitemap_generator.py:9`, `.specific()` per live page); forms (`AbstractForm`/`AbstractEmailForm` inherit Page `forms/models.py:323,390`; `AbstractFormSubmission.page` FK `:45`); routable_page (`RoutablePage` inherits Page `routable_page/models.py:221`); search_promotions (`SearchPromotion.page` FK `search_promotions/models.py:104`); simple_translation (`SubmitTranslationView` `simple_translation/views.py:20`).

---

## Step 5 — Blast radius, ranked by risk

**Tier 1 — stored page FK in own schema, drives a whole user class (silent break, migration-coupled):**
1. `Site.root_page` `sites.py:90` + `get_site_root_paths` `:218` — **every visitor request** resolves through the site→root-path map. If tree/`url_path` semantics move, all front-end serving breaks. Highest risk.
2. `Redirect.redirect_page` `redirects/models.py:33` — visitor-facing redirects stored against a page; break silently on published-URL resolution.
3. `GroupPagePermission` `pages.py:2147` + `PagePermissionPolicy` `permission_policies/pages.py:162` — editors lose (or wrongly gain) access to page subtrees; tree-scoped, driven by every admin action.
4. `PageViewRestriction` `:2470` — private-page gating for visitors; a break exposes or hides content wrongly.
5. `WorkflowPage` `:2537` / `PageSubscription` `:2843` / `PageLogEntry` `:2629` — moderation queue, notifications, audit log; editor-facing, stored FKs.
6. `AbstractFormSubmission.page` `forms/models.py:45`, `SearchPromotion.page` `search_promotions/models.py:104` — stored FKs; form submissions and promoted search results break for those apps.
7. `ReferenceIndex` `reference_index.py:290,600` — the generic stored-reference table; if the page's FK shape changes, usage/"what links here" goes stale silently.

**Tier 2 — transient per-request queries on core flows (loud, immediate):**
8. `serve` `views.py:16` + `pageurl`/`slugurl` `wagtailcore_tags.py:52` — front-end rendering; breaks loudly for every visitor.
9. `PagesAPIViewSet` `api/v2/views.py:518` — external API consumers; breaks on `live`/tree filter fields.
10. Publishing/tree actions `actions/move_page.py:21`, `publish_page_revision.py:20`, etc. — editor publish/move flows.
11. `publish_scheduled.py:29`, `set_url_paths.py:16` — scheduled cron/maintenance; break shows up asynchronously.

**Tier 3 — admin views/forms/reports** (`admin/…`): loud in the editor UI but scoped to staff; choosers, bulk actions, reports, status panel, rich-text converters.

**Tier 4 — signal handlers** (`signal_handlers.py:31`, `frontend_cache/signal_handlers.py:7`): silent — wired by `sender=Page`, no direct call site; a contract change that stops firing them degrades cache/reference sync without an error.

---

## Step 6 — Non-obvious dependents (what a text search misses)

- **`Site.get_site_root_paths` `sites.py:90/:218`** — the single most important serving dependency, yet it lives in `sites.py`, not `pages.py`, and couples to the page via cached `url_path`/tree strings rather than an obvious `Page.objects` query. Easy to miss because it reads *derived* tree state.
- **`ReferenceIndex` `reference_index.py:290`** — reaches the page **generically** through `register_model`/content-types, never naming `Page` at the query site. A grep for `Page` won't surface the model whose whole job is tracking references *to* pages.
- **Core signal handlers `signal_handlers.py:31,38,66`** — connected via `sender=Page` at `:112`; there is no direct method call, so caller-graph and text search for invocations both miss them.
- **`frontend_cache/signal_handlers.py:7,11`** — subscribes to `page_published`/`page_unpublished` by iterating `apps.get_models()` for Page subclasses (`register_signal_handlers` `:17`) — reached through **runtime model discovery + signals**, not an import of `Page`.
- **`PageLinkHandler` in three places** — `rich_text/pages.py:12`, `admin/rich_text/converters/editor_html.py:172`, `admin/rich_text/converters/html_to_contentstate.py:234` — registered as rich-text **feature handlers via configuration**, far from the model, resolving pages by stored `id` inside HTML/content-state.
- **`Redirect.redirect_page` `redirects/models.py:33`** — in a *contrib* app; stores the page ref plus a `redirect_page_route_path` string and calls `.specific_deferred`/`resolve_subpage`, coupling to routing internals unexpectedly.
- **`PageViewSetRegistry.get_content_type_id_by_page_id` `admin/viewsets/pages.py:583`** — reaches the page by **content-type id lookups**, not the ORM object; invisible to a `Page.objects` search.
- **`publish_scheduled.py:29` / `set_url_paths.py:16`** — cron/management commands (not request code); they touch `go_live_at`/`expire_at` and rewrite `url_path`, so they break silently on the next scheduled run, not in any test a reviewer runs by hand.
- **`admin/models.py:30 get_object_usage`** — walks `_meta` relations to any model whose FK target `issubclass(…, Page)` — reflective, name-free coupling.

---

## Step 7 — Change + verification map (hand-off)

| Subsystem | Dependents (file:line) | How it depends | Check when contract changes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Satellite models (stored FK)** | `GroupPagePermission` pages.py:2147; `PageViewRestriction` :2470; `WorkflowPage` :2537; `PageLogEntry` :2629; `PageSubscription` :2843; `Site.root_page` sites.py:90; `Redirect.redirect_page` redirects/models.py:33; `SearchPromotion.page` search_promotions/models.py:104; `AbstractFormSubmission.page` forms/models.py:45 | Page FK/PK in own schema | Any field moved to a satellite model needs data migration; ensure FK target unchanged; regenerate migrations; verify `on_delete` cascade semantics |

## Step 2 — The Page model contract

`Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel, metaclass=PageBase)` — `wagtail/models/pages.py:289`. `AbstractPage` (`:248`) composes the mixins that supply most of the contract:
`WorkflowMixin, PreviewableMixin, DraftStateMixin, LockableMixin, RevisionMixin, TranslatableMixin, SpecificMixin, MP_Node`.

**Own fields** (`pages.py`):
- `title` `:290`, `draft_title` `:297`, `slug` `:298`, `seo_title` `:325`, `search_description` `:342`, `show_in_menus` `:335`
- `content_type` FK→ContentType `:306` (drives `.specific`), `url_path` (text, tree-derived) `:313`
- `owner` FK→User `:314`, `alias_of` self-FK `:392` (alias mechanic), `latest_revision_created_at` `:350`
- GenericRelations: `_revisions` `:354`, `_workflow_states` `:367`, `_specific_workflow_states` `:381`

**Tree mechanics** (from `MP_Node`, treebeard materialized path): `path`, `depth`, `numchild` — used everywhere via `get_parent()`, `get_children()`, `get_descendants()`, `move()`. `set_url_path(parent)` `:577` recomputes `url_path` from tree position.

**Publishing state** (from `DraftStateMixin`, `draft_state.py:13`): `live` `:14`, `has_unpublished_changes` `:15`, `first_published_at` `:19`, `last_published_at` `:22`, `live_revision` `:25`, `go_live_at` `:35`, `expire_at` `:38`, `expired` `:41`. Behavior via `Page.publish()` `:1219`.

**i18n** (TranslatableMixin): `locale`, `translation_key`. **Locking** (LockableMixin): `locked`, `locked_by`, `locked_at`.

**Search contract:** `search_fields` `:402` enumerates the indexed shape (`title`, `live`, `owner`, `content_type`, `path`, `depth`, `locked`, `show_in_menus`, `first_published_at`, `last_published_at`, `locale`, `translation_key`…). **Copy contract:** `default_exclude_fields_in_copy` `:435` names `id/depth/numchild/url_path/path/latest_revision/comments`.

**What a teammate must know about how other code holds page references — two coupling modes:**
1. **Stored reference** — satellite models with a page FK/PK in *their own* schema (survive across requests, migration-sensitive). Sense's `composed_by`/`affected_via_composition` lists these.
2. **Transient query** — code that does `Page.objects.…` / `.specific` / tree traversal per request (breaks at runtime if field names/tree semantics move).

## Step 5 — Blast radius, ranked by risk

**Tier 1 — stored page FK in own schema, drives a whole user class (silent break, migration-coupled):**
1. `Site.root_page` `sites.py:90` + `get_site_root_paths` `:218` — **every visitor request** resolves through the site→root-path map. If tree/`url_path` semantics move, all front-end serving breaks. Highest risk.
2. `Redirect.redirect_page` `redirects/models.py:33` — visitor-facing redirects stored against a page; break silently on published-URL resolution.
3. `GroupPagePermission` `pages.py:2147` + `PagePermissionPolicy` `permission_policies/pages.py:162` — editors lose (or wrongly gain) access to page subtrees; tree-scoped, driven by every admin action.
4. `PageViewRestriction` `:2470` — private-page gating for visitors; a break exposes or hides content wrongly.
5. `WorkflowPage` `:2537` / `PageSubscription` `:2843` / `PageLogEntry` `:2629` — moderation queue, notifications, audit log; editor-facing, stored FKs.
6. `AbstractFormSubmission.page` `forms/models.py:45`, `SearchPromotion.page` `search_promotions/models.py:104` — stored FKs; form submissions and promoted search results break for those apps.
7. `ReferenceIndex` `reference_index.py:290,600` — the generic stored-reference table; if the page's FK shape changes, usage/"what links here" goes stale silently.

**Tier 2 — transient per-request queries on core flows (loud, immediate):**
8. `serve` `views.py:16` + `pageurl`/`slugurl` `wagtailcore_tags.py:52` — front-end rendering; breaks loudly for every visitor.
9. `PagesAPIViewSet` `api/v2/views.py:518` — external API consumers; breaks on `live`/tree filter fields.
10. Publishing/tree actions `actions/move_page.py:21`, `publish_page_revision.py:20`, etc. — editor publish/move flows.
11. `publish_scheduled.py:29`, `set_url_paths.py:16` — scheduled cron/maintenance; break shows up asynchronously.

**Tier 3 — admin views/forms/reports** (`admin/…`): loud in the editor UI but scoped to staff; choosers, bulk actions, reports, status panel, rich-text converters.

**Tier 4 — signal handlers** (`signal_handlers.py:31`, `frontend_cache/signal_handlers.py:7`): silent — wired by `sender=Page`, no direct call site; a contract change that stops firing them degrades cache/reference sync without an error.

---

## Step 6 — Non-obvious dependents (what a text search misses)

- **`Site.get_site_root_paths` `sites.py:90/:218`** — the single most important serving dependency, yet it lives in `sites.py`, not `pages.py`, and couples to the page via cached `url_path`/tree strings rather than an obvious `Page.objects` query. Easy to miss because it reads *derived* tree state.
- **`ReferenceIndex` `reference_index.py:290`** — reaches the page **generically** through `register_model`/content-types, never naming `Page` at the query site. A grep for `Page` won't surface the model whose whole job is tracking references *to* pages.
- **Core signal handlers `signal_handlers.py:31,38,66`** — connected via `sender=Page` at `:112`; there is no direct method call, so caller-graph and text search for invocations both miss them.
- **`frontend_cache/signal_handlers.py:7,11`** — subscribes to `page_published`/`page_unpublished` by iterating `apps.get_models()` for Page subclasses (`register_signal_handlers` `:17`) — reached through **runtime model discovery + signals**, not an import of `Page`.
- **`PageLinkHandler` in three places** — `rich_text/pages.py:12`, `admin/rich_text/converters/editor_html.py:172`, `admin/rich_text/converters/html_to_contentstate.py:234` — registered as rich-text **feature handlers via configuration**, far from the model, resolving pages by stored `id` inside HTML/content-state.
- **`Redirect.redirect_page` `redirects/models.py:33`** — in a *contrib* app; stores the page ref plus a `redirect_page_route_path` string and calls `.specific_deferred`/`resolve_subpage`, coupling to routing internals unexpectedly.
- **`PageViewSetRegistry.get_content_type_id_by_page_id` `admin/viewsets/pages.py:583`** — reaches the page by **content-type id lookups**, not the ORM object; invisible to a `Page.objects` search.
- **`publish_scheduled.py:29` / `set_url_paths.py:16`** — cron/management commands (not request code); they touch `go_live_at`/`expire_at` and rewrite `url_path`, so they break silently on the next scheduled run, not in any test a reviewer runs by hand.
- **`admin/models.py:30 get_object_usage`** — walks `_meta` relations to any model whose FK target `issubclass(…, Page)` — reflective, name-free coupling.
